result:
The original "execution completed with status=Ok but output did not match" issue is gone. New blocker is a `todo!()` panic at `table_resize`.

**Root cause:** Multiple stubs in the parse/codegen path silently dropped bytecode. `constructor()` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2343` never emitted `OP_NEWTABLE`/`OP_EXTRAARG`/`OP_SETLIST`; `closelistfield`/`lastlistfield` never discharged fields to registers; `suffixedexp`'s `[` branch never called `luaK_indexed`; and `cg_discharge_vars` didn't handle `VINDEXI/VINDEXSTR/VINDEXED`. So `local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])` compiled to just `print(<uninit local>)`, which printed an unrelated table-typed value sitting in that register.

**Fix:** In `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`, added helpers `cg_emit_newtable`, `cg_settablesize`, `cg_setlist`, `cg_indexed`, `cg_exp_to_any_reg_up`, `cg_free_reg_if_temp`; extended `cg_discharge_vars` with `IndexI`/`IndexStr`/`Indexed`/`Call`/`VarArg` arms emitting `OP_GETI`/`OP_GETFIELD`/`OP_GETTABLE`; wired `constructor`/`closelistfield`/`lastlistfield` to actually emit `OP_NEWTABLE`+`OP_EXTRAARG`, discharge fields, emit `OP_SETLIST`, and patch the array/hash sizes; wired `suffixedexp` `[` to call `cg_exp_to_any_reg_up` + `cg_indexed`.

**New error:** `not yet implemented: phase-b: table_resize` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1452` — runtime stub the loop can pick up next.
